Search results: 



--- SOURCE 1: The 2025 Sustainable Packaging Consumer Report ---
URL: https://www.shorr.com/resources/blog/sustainable-packaging-consumer-report/

SUMMARY:
The 2025 Sustainable Packaging Consumer Report | Shorr Packaging The 2025 Sustainable Packaging Consumer Report *   54% of consumers reported consciously purchasing products with sustainable packaging in the last six months. *   43% of consumers are willing to pay extra for a product with sustainable packaging. Nearly 4 in 10 consumers (39%) say they are more likely to share their purchase on social media if the product features sustainable packaging, creating valuable brand buzz. For more information on Shorr’s sustainable packaging solutions and how we help brands meet consumer demand while reducing environmental impact, visit Shorr’s Sustainability Hub. This survey explored consumer preferences for sustainable packaging, generational differences in eco-conscious behaviors, and the role of packaging in driving brand loyalty and purchase decisions.

FULL CONTENT:
Published Time: 2025-01-30T14:16:17+00:00

The 2025 Sustainable Packaging Consumer Report | Shorr Packaging

# Consumer Demand for Sustainable Packaging Is On The Rise.

![](https://www.smashbrand.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/06/Kevin.jpg)

If you could point out the “boogeyman” of the modern era, what would it be? Aside from COVID-19-which might take the title-it would probably be waste from plastics. With visions of sea turtles trapped by plastic rings and straws filling whales’ stomachs, consumers are turning their backs on the evils of plastic. Their attention has shifted toward more sustainable, environmentally friendly packaging options.

Sustainable packaging has been one of the fastest-growing trends in the packaging world. In fact, we expect the sustainable packaging market to grow at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 6% through 2027. Many companies are now pledging to use 100% recycled packaging in the next few years.

## **Does Sustainable Packaging Increase Purchase Intent?**

There is an important distinction between what shoppers “think” they want and what they actually bring to the point of purchase. Asking someone on the street differs from monitoring a potential customer as they navigate the store. A sustainable packaging design will have a differing effect based on the CPG category, customer demographic, and competitive landscape.

Keep this information in mind as we review the surveyed consumers asked about their level of interest and what they look for in a sustainable design.

With that PSA announcement, let’s now look at why there is a trend for consumers to refuse to buy products in unsustainable packaging. What is it they want? Like any good hostage situation, here’s a detailed list of their demands, so you can keep up with the sustainability trend.

## **What Customers Want from Packaging**

How do you learn what consumers want? The first step is to ask them! That is exactly what is being done in the packaging industry to establish the need and desire for sustainability in consumer packaged goods.

A recent study included consumers from ten countries. The study conductors asked how concerned they were about the environmental impact of product packaging. Approximately [55% of participants said they were extremely](https://www.mckinsey.com/industries/packaging-and-paper/our-insights/sustainability-in-packaging-inside-the-minds-of-us-consumers) concerned with the packaging material or product.

Consumers are definitely interested in sustainable packaging. Nearly 70% said they would pay more for a product in sustainable packaging, and around 35% said they would buy more sustainably packaged products if they could find them.

This means that producers who provide sustainable packaging solutions may capture a larger market share, even if they’re a bit more expensive! Of course, we should test this data in a live shopping environment to see at what price and with what purchase drivers have the greatest impact on consumer intent.

## **Potential Purchase Drivers for Sustainable Packaging**

Not all consumers understand and resonate with the same terms, even if they all lead to the same meaning. Choosing the right purchase drivers for your sustainable package design. Here are a few examples of terms that can help your product reach the point of being considered for purchase.

How do you know which triggers create purchase intent? On-shelf product testing is the only way to remove subjectivity from the process. In a realistic environment, you learn the true nature of a consumer’s desire to purchase products, such as those with recyclable packaging.

This removes the do-good ideology that people “hope” is within themselves. Instead, it focuses on when push comes to shove, are they willing to make the switch and pay the higher price? So, as you drive to include a sustainable packaging option in your design strategy, don’t assume that your results will match the interest level in these studies.

## **Factors that Impact Purchase Decisions**

Agreeing with what we just discussed, although consumers clarified they prefer sustainable packaging, it might not be *the* most important factor for shoppers when making a purchase.

They asked the participants in the study about other factors, like price and ease of use. The chart revealed some surprising conclusions when ranking all the different factors, from most important to least important. Here’s the ranking in order of importance:

As you can see, despite 55% of people being extremely or very concerned about environmental impact, this factor landed at the bottom of the list. That doesn’t mean it’s not an important factor for package design; it just means it’s not as important as the other factors in this setting.

In this study, 77% of participants put hygiene and food safety at the top of their list, 67% said shelf life, and 61% said ease of use. These ranked at a higher percentage than environmental impact. Interestingly, for many CPG products, you will find these potential purchase drivers on the back of the label, but is that where they belong?

Just because the environmental impact is bringing up the rear doesn’t mean it’s not important. Other factors, like hygiene, appearance, and ease of use, may be current considerations in your overall packaging strategy. Upon testing, the environmental impact may be the cherry on your already-exceptional package design. It’s what could separate your beautiful, safe, and easy-to-use packaging from a competitor’s beautiful, safe, and easy-to-use packaging.

## **How to Make Sustainable Packaging More Desirable to Consumers**

Based on the factors that impact buying decisions, you can’t use only sustainable packaging and expect a surge in sales. The reality of a sustainable design is that you can’t make an ugly package that falls apart the minute someone touches it. It doesn’t matter if it’s made with sustainable products; nobody will buy a product if the packaging solution doesn’t look and perform well.

Even with environmentally friendly, sustainable packaging, you still need to make your packaging attractive, safe, and easy to use, and you must also [implement the latest packaging design trends](https://www.smashbrand.com/articles/packaging-design-trends/).

Besides using the best packaging designs, you must *indicate* in the most resonating way that your packaging materials come from sustainable resources. From the same study, 36% said they would buy more sustainably packaged products if they were better labeled but if communicated incorrectly, that number may drop to 0%.

Explore as many ideas as possible to show off your sustainable packaging materials! If you are making the investment and increasing your costs & retail price, then find a marketing angle to provide immediate conversions and future brand recall.

If consumers don’t know they’re sustainable, it’s no different from the plastic packaging sitting next to it. Here are some ideas; put it in big letters, add a large sticker, or use earth tones in your design to show off how “green” your packaging is. But always test this on-shelf against a competitive landscape before concluding it is the right message for your product packaging.

## **Sustainable Packaging Trends for the Future**

It’s clear that consumers want sustainable packaging, but in such an innovative landscape, which types of sustainable packaging do they prefer? According to the study, consumers don’t yet have strong, specific preferences about the type of sustainable packaging. As long as it is sustainable, then it is acceptable.

There are, however, a few trends that will likely affect the future of sustainable packaging:

Sustainable practices are the future of product packaging and at some point will become an expectation rather than an advantage. To capitalize on the marketability of this packaging option, you need to capture the right audience, at the right time, with the right message.

To keep up with the oncoming consumer demand, consider using [biodegradable packaging](https://made-with-regen.ca/blog/biodegradable-packaging/) materials like cardboard, paper or recycled or recyclable plastic. It could be good for your sales numbers if it’s good for the environment.

## **Implement Sustainable Materials into Your Packaging Strategy**

Product packaging is a huge part of marketing; the key to good marketing is appealing to consumer interests. There’s no doubt that people have become more mindful of our environment, and that includes product packaging.

The packaging market has been moving toward sustainability for years. Gone are the days of single-use plastic. Consumers are now demanding fiber-based products and recycled materials as more sustainable solutions. If you want to give consumers what they want, it’s time to introduce sustainable materials into your strategy.

## **SmashBrand Package Design Testing Process**

If you need help with your sustainable packaging strategy, talk to the experts at SmashBrand. We help get designs off the ground, provide CPG expertise, and implement proprietary [packaging design testing](https://www.smashbrand.com/articles/packaging-design-testing-its-a-big-deal/) methods to help products stand out on the shelves.

As your sustainable package design agency, we let the testing determine the need for a sustainable approach. Talk about your next project to see if eco-friendly packaging is right for your product; [contact us](https://www.smashbrand.com/contact-us/) today.

Retailers have a unique opportunity to lead the sustainable packaging industry by leveraging sustainable packaging statistics, gathering comprehensive data, addressing internal challenges, and driving innovation.This approach not only reduces costs and strengthens supply chains but also meets consumer expectations without increasing prices. As consumer demand for eco-friendly products grows, companies must adapt their strategies to stay competitive in the evolving sustainable packaging market. Sustainability in packaging has become a critical requirement for brands as consumers increasingly demand environmentally responsible products.

# Sustainable Packaging Statistics 2025: The Impact of Eco-Friendly Solutions

![](https://meyers.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/08/haley_headshot-300x300.jpg)

Sustainability is driving significant changes across industries, including [product packaging](https://meyers.com/meyers-blog/product-packaging-guide/), as highlighted by key sustainable packaging statistics. Companies are adapting to meet [consumer demand](https://meyers.com/meyers-blog/consumer-sustainability-trends-in-the-packaging-industry/)  and regulatory requirements, recognizing that the materials and processes they choose today will significantly impact both the environment and their business success.

This change in buying behavior is largely driven by increased environmental awareness fueled by social media, documentaries, and global movements. Easy access to information has empowered consumers to make more informed choices, especially [Millennials and Gen Z](https://www.carbonneutralcopy.com/sustainable-purchases-survey/), who prioritize sustainability more than older generations.

Business owners and professionals in the procurement or packaging field need to understand sustainability and how it can impact their brand. Aligning with these evolving expectations is crucial for maintaining market relevance and enhancing brand loyalty.

This article provides valuable insights into the sustainable packaging industry and key sustainable packaging statistics that explain why integrating eco-friendly practices into your packaging strategies is essential to meet consumer demands while contributing to broader sustainability goals.

![](https://meyers.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/sustainable_packaging-1.jpg)

## Importance of **Sustainable Packaging and Its Environmental Impact**

Many brands are transitioning to sustainable packaging due to its positive environmental benefits. Here are some examples of how sustainable packaging helps conserve the planet and its resources.

### **1. Reduction of solid waste**

Packaging waste is a major contributor to municipal solid waste, with the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) [reporting that in 2018](https://www.epa.gov/facts-and-figures-about-materials-waste-and-recycling/containers-and-packaging-product-specific), packaging and containers made up 82.2 million tons or 28.1% of the total waste generated.

By adopting recyclable and reusable materials, brands can help reduce the volume of waste ending up in landfills. For instance, using packaging made from [post-consumer recycled (PCR) content](https://meyers.com/meyers-blog/what-is-pcr-plastic-pcr-packaging-applications/) not only diverts waste from landfills but also reduces the demand for virgin materials.

### **2. Decreased reliance on fossil fuels**

The production of traditional plastic packaging is heavily dependent on fossil fuels, with [99% of plastic products requiring them](https://www.nrdc.org/bio/renee-sharp/hidden-fossil-fuels-plastic-production-drives-climate-change). The extraction and burning of fossil fuels release significant amounts of carbon dioxide, a major greenhouse gas contributing to global warming.

By switching to sustainable packaging options such as [bioplastics](https://www.meyers.com/meyers-blog/what-are-bioplastics-how-sustainable-are-they/), which are derived from renewable resources like cornstarch or sugarcane, companies can reduce their carbon footprint. Moreover, the use of recycled plastics in packaging further cuts down on the need for new fossil fuel-based materials, helping to mitigate climate change.

### **3. Conservation of natural resources**

Adopting a [minimalist approach to packaging design](https://meyers.com/meyers-blog/minimalist-design-ideas-for-product-labels-and-packaging/) or opting for renewable, biodegradable, or compostable options can help reduce negative environmental impact. For example, packaging made from bamboo, a rapidly renewable resource, helps preserve forests and reduces the depletion of non-renewable resources like petroleum-based plastics. This approach underscores the importance of sustainable packaging in ensuring that resources are available for future generations.

### **4. Increased energy efficiency**

Traditional packaging production often requires significant energy inputs, primarily from fossil fuels. However, manufacturers are increasingly turning to renewable energy sources like solar and wind to power their operations. This shift not only reduces the carbon footprint associated with packaging production but also promotes energy independence and sustainability.

Additionally, the manufacturing processes for sustainable materials, such as recycled aluminum or glass, typically require less energy than producing these materials from scratch, further enhancing energy efficiency.

### **5. Extended shelf life**

Sustainable packaging solutions also offer practical benefits, such as extending the shelf life of products, particularly food items. Innovative packaging materials like low-density perforated polyethylene (LDPE) can significantly prolong the freshness of produce, such as extending the shelf life of bananas to [up to 30 days](https://www.mdpi.com/2304-8158/11/8/1086/pdf?version=1649662615).

This technology enhances durability and product protection, minimizes the frequency of product spoilage and damage, and contributes to less waste in both retail and consumer settings.

## **7 Key Sustainable Packaging Statistics**

### **1. The sustainable packaging market is projected to grow to $423.56 billion by 2029**

The sustainable packaging market is projected to grow from an estimated USD 292.71 billion in 2024 [to USD 423.56 billion by 2029](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/sustainable-packaging-market), reflecting a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 7.67%. Key factors driving this expansion include rising consumer interest in sustainability, the growing influence of circular economy practices, and stricter global regulations aimed at reducing packaging waste, particularly single-use plastics.

![Sustainable Packaging Market Globally](https://meyers.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/sustainable_packaging_market.jpg)

Source: [Mordor Intelligence](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/sustainable-packaging-market)

Policies like the European Union’s Circular Economy Action Plan and mandates to reduce plastic waste in countries such as the United States and Canada are pushing companies to invest more in sustainable packaging solutions.

Countries such as France, Germany, and the U.K. are leading with robust recycling measures and [extended producer responsibilities (EPRs)](https://www.meyers.com/meyers-blog/extended-producer-responsibility-epr-packaging/). In Asia, Thailand has implemented a nationwide ban on single-use plastic bags to combat plastic pollution.

Despite advancements, plastic pollution remains a critical issue, with 8 million metric tons of plastic entering the oceans annually. By 2025, over 40% of companies plan to adopt innovative and sustainable packaging techniques as they pivot towards a circular economy.

However, the continued use of non-recyclable and non-biodegradable plastics is exacerbating carbon emissions. In response, major corporations such as Amazon, Google, and Tetrapak are setting ambitious net-zero carbon targets, a move that will require significant capital investments.

### **2. Food and beverage is the leading segment in the global sustainable packaging market in terms of application**

The food and beverages sector [leads the global green packaging market](https://www.fortunebusinessinsights.com/green-packaging-market-105113), primarily driven by the demand for convenience foods such as frozen items, snacks, and beverages. These products are popular due to their portability, long shelf life, and ease of preparation.

![Sustainable Packaging Statistics for the Beverage Market](https://meyers.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/sustainable_beverage_packaging.jpg)

[Source](https://www.shutterstock.com/image-photo/african-man-shopping-beverage-section-supermarket-665241124)

Consumers are increasingly choosing convenience foods that provide nutritional value, appealing packaging, and safety. Drivenby busy lifestyles and an aging population, this demand has led packaging manufacturers to focus on innovative, sustainable solutions.

As consumer awareness of sustainability increases, chemical suppliers and FMCG companies are moving toward greener packaging by reducing plastic use and enhancing packaging structures. Regulatory measures and industry commitments are further accelerating this transition to sustainable packaging.

### **3. The recycled materials packaging solutions market size is currently valued at $189.92 billion**

The [recycled materials packaging solutions market](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/global-recycled-materials-packaging-solutions-packaging-solutions-industry) is projected to reach USD 245.56 billion by 2029 at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 5.27%. In terms of production volume, the market is expected to increase from 248.60 million tons to 315.70 million tons during the same period.

Recycled materials, reprocessed from previously used products, are becoming a crucial component in [sustainable packaging](https://meyers.com/meyers-blog/guide-to-sustainable-packaging-for-business/). Companies are increasingly turning to recycled content to reduce waste and optimize manufacturing processes, offering both environmental and cost benefits.

![](https://meyers.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/recycled_materials_packaging.jpg)

Source: [Mordor Intelligence](https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/global-recycled-materials-packaging-solutions-packaging-solutions-industry)

One key area of growth within this market is the adoption of [post-consumer recycled (PCR) materials](https://meyers.com/meyers-blog/what-is-pcr-packaging-types-benefits-applications/). [PCR packaging](https://meyers.com/capabilities/packaging/pcr-packaging/) involves recycling materials like plastics collected from consumer products and reprocessing them into new packaging. This approach not only decreases reliance on virgin materials but also contributes to a circular economy by reintroducing used materials into the production cycle.

Technological advancements have led to innovative recyclable packaging solutions that meet industry needs while ensuring product protection and functionality. The [rise of biodegradable and bio-based materials](https://meyers.com/meyers-blog/latest-biodegradable-packaging-industry-trends/) further supports sustainability efforts by reducing the environmental impact associated with traditional oil-based polymers.

Furthermore, promoting recycling helps reduce waste management costs, allowing municipalities to allocate savings to other critical areas such as education and infrastructure.

![](https://meyers.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/post_consumer_recycled_packaging.jpg)

### **4. ESG-claimed products outpaced those without such claims, achieving a 28% average growth over five years**

A [McKinsey & Company report](https://www.mckinsey.com/industries/consumer-packaged-goods/our-insights/consumers-care-about-sustainability-and-back-it-up-with-their-wallets) showed that products with ESG-related claims saw an average growth of 28% over five years, compared to 20% for non-ESG products. These statistics reflect a strong consumer preference for products with environmental, social, and governance (ESG) attributes.

Consequently, brands must innovate by incorporating sustainable materials and practices to enhance their brand reputation and drive market growth. However, the report also reveals that not all ESG claims have the same impact. Less common claims, like “vegan” or “carbon zero,” tend to drive higher growth compared to more common claims, such as “environmentally sustainable.”

To gain a competitive advantage, brands should focus on unique and meaningful ESG claims, ensuring they are backed by genuine actions to build consumer trust and loyalty.

### **5. While U.S. consumers are willing to pay an 11% premium for eco-friendly products, the actual premium averages 28%.**

As concerns rise, consumers are increasingly willing to pay more for sustainable products but often encounter barriers. According to Bain & Company, half of consumers consider sustainability a top purchase criterion, yet misconceptions about what is truly sustainable [often lead to misguided choices](https://www.bain.com/insights/selling-sustainability-means-decoding-consumers-ceo-sustainability-guide-2023/).

Many consumers also differ in their definitions of sustainability, with 50% focusing on product reuse and waste minimization, while companies tend to market sustainability based on production methods and ingredients.

![Sustainable Packaging Statistics in the U.S.](https://meyers.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/sustainable_packaging_costs.jpg)

This disconnect results in nearly half of consumers in developed markets believing sustainable living is too costly, compared to 35% in fast-growing markets. Key sustainable packaging statistics reflect how cultural norms and financial realities heavily influence these perceptions. In fast-growing markets, sustainability often aligns with traditional practices, while in developed markets, it requires resisting a consumption-driven culture.

Consumer behavior can shift rapidly, influenced by external factors like government regulations. For example, China’s incentives for electric vehicles have led 19% of its consumers to drive electric cars, compared to 8% globally. Since the introduction of the single-use plastic bag charge in England in 2015, their usage has dropped by 98%.

### **6. Only 7% of companies succeed in sustainability transformations**

Achieving major sustainability transformations is challenging, with [only 7% of companies succeeding](https://www.bain.com/insights/sustainable-packaging-paper-and-packaging-report-2023/). For those pursuing packaging circularity, it’s crucial to focus on five key areas: benchmarks, legislation, infrastructure, consumer behavior, and technology, collectively known as BLICT.

![](https://meyers.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/sustainable_packaging_solutions.jpg)

Source: [Bain & Company](https://www.bain.com/insights/sustainable-packaging-paper-and-packaging-report-2023/)

Understanding these key principles, companies can prioritize efforts and engage stakeholders. One global beauty brand used this approach to reduce its packaging-related greenhouse gas emissions by over 40% by 2030. This brand achieved this result by reducing packaging material by 20%, incorporating 50% recycled plastic, and ensuring all packaging is reusable, recyclable, or compostable.

### **7. The demand for recycled plastics can outstrip supply by 2030**

A common misconception about sustainable packaging is that it is always more expensive than traditional materials. However, the reality is more nuanced. For instance, if all retailers meet their sustainability targets, the demand for recycled plastics [could reach 90 million tons by 2030](https://www.mckinsey.com/industries/retail/our-insights/reimagine-reuse-recycle-how-to-reach-sustainable-packaging-targets-in-retail), while the global supply may only hit 60 million tons.

This disparity could drive up costs, affecting retailer margins. Although there is often a premium on recycled materials, retailers can lower these costs by investing in capabilities and engaging upstream in the value chain. By doing so, they can reduce their plastic packaging expenses by an average of 15% and up to 40% in some categories.

![Sustainable Packaging Statistics](https://meyers.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/How-to-achieve-sustainable-packaging-in-retail-1024x610.jpg)

Source: [McKinsey & Company](https://www.mckinsey.com/industries/retail/our-insights/reimagine-reuse-recycle-how-to-reach-sustainable-packaging-targets-in-retail)

Retailers have a unique opportunity to lead the sustainable packaging industry by leveraging sustainable packaging statistics, gathering comprehensive data, addressing internal challenges, and driving innovation.This approach not only reduces costs and strengthens supply chains but also meets consumer expectations without increasing prices.

Building a sustainable packaging strategy involves understanding the entire packaging portfolio, integrating sustainability principles into design processes, forming partnerships across the value chain, and developing new internal capabilities. This holistic approach is crucial for aligning with the broader goals of the sustainable packaging market in today’s economy.

## **Importance of Sustainable Packaging: Strategic Implications for Brands**

![](https://meyers.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/sustainable_packaging.jpg)

The sustainable packaging statistics highlight opportunities and challenges for brands. As consumer demand for eco-friendly products grows, companies must adapt their strategies to stay competitive in the evolving sustainable packaging market. Here are key considerations.

### **1. Build a resilient and adaptable packaging strategy**

Consumer priorities are changing faster than many companies can keep up with, making it crucial for businesses to look beyond the traditional 3-year strategic planning window. While some companies plan, they often focus on technology adoption, neglecting other rapidly evolving factors like regulations and consumer behavior.

To stay competitive, companies must develop strategies that are not only flexible but also future-proof, addressing these critical elements.

### **2. Proactively navigate and influence regulatory developments**

Government regulations are increasingly shaping consumer behavior and market dynamics, as reflected in key sustainable packaging statistics. To remain competitive, companies must proactively anticipate and influence these regulatory developments. Collaborating with an experienced [product packaging manufacturer](https://meyers.com/capabilities/packaging/) can help businesses navigate the complexities of evolving regulations and ensure their packaging strategies are compliant and future-proof.

As government intervention becomes more aggressive in response to global warming, anticipating policy shifts and adapting accordingly will be crucial for maintaining a competitive edge.

### **3. Utilize digital tools for ongoing experimentation and adjustment**

In a dynamic market, continued growth is essential. Companies should use digital tools to test and refine their sustainability messaging, gathering deep insights into what resonates with various consumer segments. Brands can quickly adapt their strategies based on real-time data. This strategy can help align their offerings with consumer values while boosting marketing ROI and driving sales growth.

### **4. Understand and address a diverse consumer landscape**

Traditional assumptions about sustainability-focused consumers are outdated. Today’s market is diverse, with varied definitions of sustainability and motivations for their purchasing decisions. Companies must innovate to create products and packaging that resonate with these diverse groups.

For instance, custom packaging solutions allow brands to design packaging that aligns with local market preferences and the unique sustainability values of different consumer segments. This approach ensures that sustainability is strategically aligned with key purchase criteria without forcing trade-offs.

## **Giving Green a Chance**

Sustainability in packaging has become a critical requirement for brands as consumers increasingly demand environmentally responsible products. Key sustainable packaging statistics highlight the harmful effects of unsustainable packaging, driving consumers to expect companies to adopt practices that protect the environment.

For brands, transitioning to sustainable packaging is not just about meeting consumer expectations—it’s about aligning with global sustainability goals and enhancing brand reputation. However, this shift can be complex, requiring specialized knowledge and expertise.

Meyers understands the challenges and opportunities in making this transition. enriched_points.expanded_content: Over 54% of consumers prioritize purchasing products with sustainable packaging, reflecting a significant shift towards eco-friendly choices that enhance brand loyalty and social media visibility.
Stricter regulations globally aim to reduce packaging waste, particularly single-use plastics, compelling brands to adopt sustainable practices and innovate in their packaging solutions.

Companies are increasingly recognizing the importance of sustainability in packaging as a critical aspect of corporate responsibility, aligning their practices with consumer expectations and environmental goals.
